options.text: Commence oral di/flucloxacillin 25 mg/kg six-hourly for 7 days and refer to ENT for elective complete sinus excision once the acute infection has fully settled
Refer urgently for total parotidectomy under suspicion of an infected first branchial cleft anomaly with facial nerve involvement
Reassure the family that the swelling is self-limiting and arrange follow-up review only if the lesion enlarges or discharges
Express the sinus contents by squeezing the lesion firmly today and apply topical mupirocin 2% ointment three times daily for 7 days
Commence oral aciclovir 20 mg/kg five times daily for 7 days as empirical treatment for suspected herpes zoster oticus
Refer urgently to paediatric ophthalmology for examination under general anaesthesia and consideration of strabismus surgery despite normal Hirschberg and cover-uncover testing in the rooms today

Reassure parents that this is pseudoesotropia from epicanthic folds, document normal Hirschberg and cover-uncover testing, and advise to return for review if any change is noted or by the routine 4 year preschool vision screen
Patch the left eye with an adhesive eye patch for 2 hours daily for 3 months to prevent amblyopia despite absent strabismus or amblyopia on objective testing today
Commence topical atropine 1% drops once daily to the right eye to penalise the dominant eye and treat presumed amblyopia in the left eye without confirmed specialist diagnosis
Order an outpatient MRI brain and orbits under sedation to exclude an intracranial mass causing a sixth cranial nerve palsy despite the full painless eye movements documented today
ECT is absolutely contraindicated because of his prior inferior myocardial infarction
ECT is a first-line treatment for severe MDD with psychotic features and food refusal
ECT cannot lawfully proceed because he currently lacks capacity to consent
All antidepressants must be ceased before ECT to avoid serotonin syndrome
Cognitive screening is unnecessary because he has no documented dementia diagnosis
Commence olanzapine 5 mg nocte and arrange GP follow-up in two weeks
Refer to the local early-psychosis (headspace / EPPIC-aligned) service for assertive multidisciplinary assessment and treatment planning
Diagnose cannabis-induced psychotic disorder and discharge with a brief intervention on cessation
Arrange an outpatient MRI brain and EEG before any psychiatric treatment is offered
Admit involuntarily under the relevant state Mental Health Act for assessment
Continue daily thiamine 100 mg orally and observe with hourly CIWA-Ar reassessment overnight
Symptom-triggered diazepam 10 to 20 mg PO 1- to 2-hourly titrated to CIWA-Ar plus IV Pabrinex
Commence oral carbamazepine 200 mg three times daily as the sole withdrawal agent
Use an intravenous propofol infusion in a ward-based setting with bedside monitoring
Restart small amounts of oral alcohol to taper consumption over 5 days at home
IV high-dose B-complex (e.g. Pabrinex 2 pairs IV TDS), correct magnesium, then continue dextrose
Continue dextrose, give a single 100 mg dose of intramuscular thiamine and monitor
Hold any further glucose and commence intravenous magnesium sulfate as monotherapy
Arrange an urgent MRI brain before commencing any pharmacological treatment
Commence oral thiamine 300 mg daily without any parenteral B-vitamin therapy
Add risperidone 1 mg BD scheduled and continue oxycodone, lorazepam, and metoclopramide
Start donepezil 5 mg daily for worsening Alzheimer disease and discharge home
Transfer to closed psychiatric ward under involuntary order for management of psychosis
Minimise deliriogenic medications, address constipation, optimise non-pharmacological measures, treat UTI only if clinically supported
Commence IV ciprofloxacin empirically for presumed UTI based on urinalysis
Add lithium augmentation titrated to trough level 0.4 to 0.8 mmol/L with renal and thyroid monitoring
Switch venlafaxine to fluoxetine 20 mg daily monotherapy without augmentation
Add bupropion 150 mg daily as monoamine combination augmentation
Add diazepam 5 mg BD as anxiolytic augmentation for six weeks
Refer urgently for ECT as first-line treatment for resistant depression
Anticholinergic toxidrome from antimuscarinic overdose
Neuroleptic malignant syndrome from antipsychotic exposure
Serotonin syndrome from triple serotonergic combination (escitalopram, tramadol, linezolid)
Sympathomimetic toxidrome from stimulant intoxication
Malignant hyperthermia from anaesthetic exposure
Add sodium valproate 500 mg BD as adjunct mood stabiliser
Add quetiapine extended-release titrated to 300-600 mg nocte with metabolic monitoring
Switch lithium to carbamazepine 200 mg BD
Stop lithium immediately because of pregnancy planning
Switch lithium to lamotrigine 200 mg daily as monotherapy
IV sodium bicarbonate 1-2 mmol/kg bolus, repeated to narrow QRS and target pH 7.50-7.55
IV flumazenil 0.2 mg to reverse sedation
IV amiodarone 300 mg loading dose for QRS prolongation
Gastric lavage and oral activated charcoal 50 g
IV physostigmine 1 mg slow push to reverse anticholinergic effects
Admit involuntarily under the Mental Health Act for inpatient stabilisation
Discharge with a 'no-suicide contract' signed by the patient
Collaboratively develop a Stanley-Brown style safety plan with the patient and contact her DBT team
Commence olanzapine 10 mg daily for affective instability
Refer to drug and alcohol service for substance-use treatment
Watchful waiting for grief resolution over 3-6 months with no specific treatment
Diagnose prolonged grief disorder and offer grief therapy alone
Start a benzodiazepine for short-term anxiolysis and review in 4 weeks
Sertraline 25-50 mg daily plus structured psychotherapy with perinatal-experienced clinician and grief-informed peer support
Recommend immediate next pregnancy as therapeutic to resolve mood symptoms
Discharge home with mother, written safety plan, and outpatient youth mental health referral
Admit (voluntary or involuntary) to an adolescent mental health unit, remove home means, and link with LGBTQAI+ youth supports
Commence fluoxetine 10 mg daily tonight in ED and discharge home
Notify child protection services and remove young person from home
Reassure as 'normal teenage stress' and arrange GP follow-up next week
Administer salbutamol 12 puffs (100 mcg/puff) via MDI and spacer with tidal breathing, add ipratropium bromide 4 puffs (21 mcg/puff) via MDI and spacer, and give oral prednisolone 1 mg/kg immediately

Commence oxygen via Hudson mask at 8 L/min to target SpO2 >94%, administer nebulised salbutamol 5 mg plus ipratropium bromide 250 mcg via oxygen-driven nebuliser, give oral prednisolone 1 mg/kg, and repeat salbutamol nebulisers every 20 minutes for the first hour
Commence high-flow nasal prong oxygen at 2 L/kg/min, administer IV hydrocortisone 4 mg/kg, and arrange an urgent chest X-ray to exclude pneumonia before commencing bronchodilator therapy
Administer IV magnesium sulphate 50 mg/kg over 20 minutes as first-line therapy, followed by oral prednisolone 1 mg/kg, and commence oxygen via nasal prongs at 2 L/min to maintain SpO2 >92%
Administer nebulised salbutamol 2.5 mg alone via oxygen-driven nebuliser, give oral prednisolone 1 mg/kg, and observe for 1 hour; if no improvement, add ipratropium and consider IV therapy
Intravenous amoxicillin-clavulanate 25 mg/kg 8-hourly for suspected bacterial lymphadenitis and scarlet fever
High-dose aspirin 30-50 mg/kg/day in four divided doses alone for 7 days, with echocardiogram after treatment
Intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIG) 2 g/kg as a single infusion PLUS high-dose aspirin 30-50 mg/kg/day in four divided doses, with transition to low-dose aspirin after defervescence
Intravenous methylprednisolone 30 mg/kg/day for 3 days as first-line therapy to rapidly reduce inflammation, reserving IVIG for non-responders
Observe and discharge with oral ibuprofen 10 mg/kg 8-hourly, as the illness is likely a benign viral exanthem that will resolve spontaneously
Refer to paediatric nephrology for renal biopsy to confirm minimal change disease before commencing therapy
Commence oral prednisolone 60 mg/m²/day (approximately 40 mg daily) in divided doses for 4 weeks, then 40 mg/m² on alternate days for 4 weeks, with a low-salt diet and daily urine protein monitoring
Commence oral furosemide 1 mg/kg twice daily and a strict fluid restriction of 800 mL/day to manage the oedema, with no corticosteroid therapy
Administer intravenous 20% albumin 1 g/kg over 4 hours followed by IV furosemide 1 mg/kg, then commence oral prednisolone after oedema has resolved
Start oral enalapril 0.1 mg/kg daily to reduce proteinuria and observe for spontaneous resolution, as post-infectious glomerulonephritis is the most likely cause in this age group
Give a 20 mL/kg bolus of 0.9% sodium chloride over 15 minutes, repeat once if shock persists, then start rehydration at 10 mL/kg/hour with 0.45% sodium chloride and added potassium

Give a 10 mL/kg bolus of 0.9% sodium chloride over 30 minutes, then calculate the deficit (approximately 14% x 28 kg ≈ 4 L) and replace 50% of deficit plus maintenance over the first 24 hours, 50% over the next 24 hours, using 0.9% sodium chloride with 40 mmol/L potassium chloride once urine output is confirmed
Administer a rapid 40 mL/kg bolus of 0.9% sodium chloride over 1 hour to restore circulating volume, then calculate deficit and replace over 24 hours with 0.9% sodium chloride
Commence an IV insulin infusion at 0.1 units/kg/hour immediately before any fluid bolus, and give maintenance fluids with 0.9% sodium chloride and 5% dextrose
Start intravenous 0.9% sodium chloride at maintenance rate (40 mL/hour) plus a 10 mL/kg bolus, and commence IV sodium bicarbonate 1 mmol/kg to rapidly correct the severe acidosis
Oral polyethylene glycol 3350 (Movicol) 2 sachets/kg/day with increased fluids and continue pancreatic enzymes
Oral Gastrografin (diatrizoate meglumine) 50 mL diluted in 200 mL of water or juice, followed by intravenous fluids and nasogastric decompression if vomiting persists
Nasogastric N-acetylcysteine 10 mL of 20% solution four times daily to break down the mucus plugs
Lactulose 10 mL three times daily and a phosphate enema per rectum to stimulate colonic evacuation
Intravenous metoclopramide 0.15 mg/kg 8-hourly and continuous nasogastric suction with nil by mouth for 48 hours
Feed the infant breast milk or formula, and recheck BGL in 30 minutes; if <2.6 mmol/L, give buccal dextrose 40% gel 200 mg/kg
Give an IV bolus of 2 mL/kg of 10% dextrose immediately
Observe and repeat BGL in 2 hours
Commence an IV infusion of 10% dextrose at 60 mL/kg/day
Administer intramuscular glucagon 0.1 mg/kg
Commence oral morphine sulphate 0.4 mg/kg/day in divided doses, titrated to Finnegan scores
Commence phenobarbitone 5 mg/kg/day
Administer a single dose of IV naloxone 0.1 mg/kg
Provide supportive care only (swaddling, low stimulation, small frequent feeds)
Commence oral clonidine 1 mcg/kg 6-hourly
Slit-lamp examination by an ophthalmologist to screen for chronic anterior uveitis
MRI of the right knee with contrast
Echocardiogram to assess for pericarditis
Anti-streptolysin O titre to exclude acute rheumatic fever
Bone marrow aspirate to exclude leukaemia
0.45% sodium chloride with 5% dextrose and 20 mmol/L potassium chloride at maintenance rate
Ringer's lactate solution at 1.5 times maintenance rate
0.9% sodium chloride boluses of 20 mL/kg over 20–30 minutes until perfusion improves, followed by 0.9% sodium chloride with 5% dextrose at maintenance rate
0.9% sodium chloride with 5% dextrose and 40 mmol/L potassium chloride at maintenance rate
5% dextrose with 0.2% sodium chloride at twice maintenance rate
Administer intravenous immunoglobulin 2 g/kg to clear maternal autoantibodies

Monitor the infant closely for signs of low cardiac output or heart failure, and refer to paediatric cardiology for consideration of permanent pacemaker insertion if the bradycardia persists or becomes symptomatic
Start oral prednisolone 2 mg/kg/day to suppress the autoimmune-mediated injury to the conduction system
Observe without specific monitoring, as the heart block typically resolves spontaneously within the first 6 months
Insert a temporary transvenous pacemaker immediately to increase the ventricular rate to 100 bpm
Commence oral trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole and arrange outpatient renal ultrasound
Admit for intravenous ampicillin and gentamicin after blood and urine cultures, and perform a renal ultrasound during admission
Obtain a catheter urine specimen to confirm the diagnosis before starting antibiotics
Treat for viral illness with antipyretics and review in 24 hours
Perform a lumbar puncture before starting antibiotics
Non-surgical reduction via air enema under fluoroscopic guidance
Emergency laparotomy with manual reduction
Observation with serial abdominal examinations
Diagnostic laparoscopy with reduction
Intravenous antibiotics and bowel rest
Oral polyethylene glycol 3350 (Movicol) 2 sachets/kg/day with increased fluids
Oral Gastrografin (diatrizoate meglumine) 50–100 mL diluted in water or soft drink, with IV fluids and NG decompression if vomiting persists
N-acetylcysteine 10 mL of 20% solution via nasogastric tube four times daily as sole therapy
Lactulose 10 mL three times daily and a phosphate enema
Intravenous metoclopramide 0.15 mg/kg 8-hourly and nil by mouth for 48 hours

Commence intravenous N-acetylcysteine (NAC) according to the three-bag protocol (150 mg/kg over 1 hour, then 50 mg/kg over 4 hours, then 100 mg/kg over 16 hours) and monitor liver function tests and INR
Activate oral charcoal 50 g as the ingestion was within 2 hours, then reassess the need for N-acetylcysteine
Observe without treatment, as the ALT and INR are currently normal, and repeat blood tests in 12 hours to detect any delayed hepatotoxicity
Refer for urgent liver transplantation evaluation, as the high paracetamol level and early symptoms indicate irreversible hepatic necrosis
Administer intravenous pantoprazole 40 mg for gastric protection and arrange psychiatric review for the intentional overdose
Commence a tumour necrosis factor (TNF) inhibitor, such as infliximab 5 mg/kg IV at 0, 2, and 6 weeks, then every 8 weeks, combined with an immunomodulator
Increase azathioprine dose to 3 mg/kg/day and continue mesalazine, as the fistula will heal with medical therapy alone
Perform a fistulotomy and primary closure under general anaesthesia, and continue azathioprine
Insert a Seton drain and treat with oral metronidazole 10 mg/kg three times daily for 6 months, without escalation of immunosuppression
Refer for a diverting colostomy, as perianal Crohn's disease is refractory to medical therapy
Intravenous midazolam infusion 0.1–0.2 mg/kg/hr, titrated to seizure suppression, with consideration of thiopentone if ineffective
Intravenous phenytoin 20 mg/kg over 20 minutes
Intravenous propofol 2 mg/kg bolus followed by infusion
Intramuscular paraldehyde 0.4 mL/kg
Intravenous magnesium sulphate 50 mg/kg over 20 minutes
Administer intravenous mannitol 0.5–1 g/kg over 20 minutes or hypertonic saline 3% 5 mL/kg over 10 minutes, and arrange an urgent CT brain
Stop the insulin infusion, administer 2 mL/kg of 50% dextrose IV, and give hydrocortisone 50 mg IV
Intubate immediately for airway protection and hyperventilate to a pCO₂ of 25–30 mmHg to reduce intracranial pressure
Increase the rate of normal saline infusion to 20 mL/kg/hr to improve cerebral perfusion
Reduce the insulin infusion to 0.05 units/kg/hr and monitor for improvement
Superior mesenteric artery (SMA) syndrome
Chronic duodenal atresia
Annular pancreas
Eosinophilic oesophagitis
Celiac artery compression syndrome
Administer intravenous 3% hypertonic saline 5 mL/kg over 20 minutes, and position the head midline with 30-degree elevation
Give intravenous mannitol 1 g/kg over 20 minutes
Immediately hyperventilate to a pCO₂ of 25–30 mmHg to reduce cerebral blood volume
Insert an intracranial pressure monitor and administer thiopentone coma
Administer intravenous dexamethasone 0.5 mg/kg to reduce cerebral oedema
Switch to adalimumab (another anti-TNF agent) with loading dose 160 mg, then 80 mg at 2 weeks, then 40 mg every 2 weeks, and consider adding methotrexate as an immunomodulator
Increase the infliximab dose to 10 mg/kg every 4 weeks and continue azathioprine
Switch to ustekinumab (an anti-IL-12/23 agent) as a first-line alternative to anti-TNF therapy
Add oral prednisolone 40 mg daily to suppress the flare and continue the same infliximab regimen
Stop all biologic therapy and refer for elective colectomy
Commence intravenous methylprednisolone 1 g daily for 5 days plus intravenous immunoglobulin 2 g/kg over 5 days, and arrange urgent laparoscopic oophorectomy for the ovarian teratoma
Start oral risperidone 2 mg twice daily for psychosis and schedule an outpatient psychiatric review
Initiate intravenous aciclovir 10 mg/kg 8-hourly for presumed herpes simplex virus encephalitis until the CSF PCR result is available
Perform therapeutic plasma exchange alone as the sole immunomodulatory therapy, without corticosteroids, to avoid steroid-related psychiatric worsening
Administer intravenous rituximab 375 mg/m² weekly for 4 weeks as first-line therapy
Wilson disease presenting as acute liver failure with Coombs-negative haemolytic anaemia; measure serum ceruloplasmin, total serum copper, and 24-hour urinary copper
Autoimmune hepatitis; test for anti-nuclear antibody, anti-smooth muscle antibody, and liver-kidney microsomal type 1 antibody
Acute paracetamol toxicity; send a serum paracetamol level and commence N-acetylcysteine
Hepatitis A infection; test for hepatitis A IgM antibodies
Budd-Chiari syndrome; arrange a Doppler ultrasound of the hepatic veins
Rapid splitting of the treating team into all-good and all-bad
Multiple low-lethality self-harm episodes since adolescence
Two emergency presentations after relationship arguments
Chronic feelings of emptiness for many years
Tearful affect that brightens within the interview
Rigid preoccupation with order and control
Hoarding of work documents at home
Reluctance to delegate tasks to others
Long working hours with high productivity
Behaviours experienced as appropriate, not as intrusive or distressing
Risperidone 0.25 mg BD reviewed at 12 weeks for ongoing need
Olanzapine 10 mg nocte continued indefinitely
Haloperidol 2 mg BD for delusional content
Quetiapine 100 mg BD as first-line for psychosis
Lorazepam 1 mg TDS scheduled for agitation
Restrict cow milk and start oral iron 3 mg/kg/day, recheck in 4 weeks
Refer for inpatient packed red cell transfusion given Hb <90 g/L
Order haemoglobin electrophoresis and HbA2 quantification before starting any treatment
Day-stay IV iron polymaltose infusion now in the haematology unit
Multivitamin containing 5 mg elemental iron daily, continue current milk intake
Commence oral levothyroxine 100 microg daily and recheck TFTs in 6-8 weeks
Commence carbimazole 15 mg daily and propranolol 20 mg three times daily
Arrange thyroid ultrasound and fine-needle aspiration of the goitre before any treatment
Defer treatment for 3 months to allow for possible spontaneous resolution of subclinical disease
Commence oral levothyroxine 50 microg daily (about 1.2-1.7 microg/kg) and recheck TFTs in 6-8 weeks
Primary herpes simplex gingivostomatitis with secondary autoinoculation to the hands
Varicella with mucosal involvement and acral spread from secondary scratching
Hand-foot-and-mouth disease due to enterovirus, most often coxsackievirus A16 subtype
Herpangina due to coxsackievirus with secondary autoinoculation to the hands
Scabies with secondary bacterial impetiginisation of the wrists and feet
Remove central line immediately, culture tip before any antibiotics
Paired cultures plus IV meropenem and vancomycin with 20 mL/kg saline bolus, within 60 minutes
CT abdomen-pelvis to exclude typhlitis before commencing antibiotics
IV piperacillin-tazobactam alone, reassess at 30 minutes before escalating
Give granulocyte colony-stimulating factor immediately to raise neutrophils
Transfuse one paediatric unit of packed red cells
Refer for bone marrow aspirate to exclude leukaemia
Haemoglobin electrophoresis and faecal occult blood first-line before iron
Oral elemental iron, reduce cow's milk under 500 mL, recheck FBC 4 weeks
Arrange outpatient IV iron infusion as the first-line treatment
